tools.name: schedule_create
tools.description: Create a scheduled automation. For recurring: provide expression (cron/RRULE). For one-time: provide fire_at (ISO 8601 timestamp). ONLY use this when the user explicitly wants something to run on a schedule (e.g. "every day at 9am", "remind me tomorrow at 3pm", "weekly on Mondays"). Do NOT use this for "add to my tasks" or "add to my queue" - use task_list_add for those requests instead.

name.description: A human-readable name for the scheduled task

syntax.description: Schedule syntax type for recurring schedules. Auto-detected if omitted. Ignored when fire_at is provided.

expression.description: Schedule expression (cron or RRULE) for recurring schedules. Not needed when fire_at is provided.

fire_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for a one-time schedule (e.g. "2025-06-15T09:00:00Z"). Must be in the future. When provided, expression/syntax are ignored.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles"). Defaults to system timezone if omitted.

message.description: The message to send to the assistant when the schedule triggers. Required for execute and notify modes.

script.description: The shell command to run when the schedule triggers. Required for script mode. Runs in the workspace directory with a sanitized environment.

enabled.description: Whether the job is enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

mode.description: Whether to notify the user, execute autonomously via LLM, or run a shell command directly. Defaults to "execute".

routing_intent.description: How to route the triggered message across channels. Defaults to "all_channels".

routing_hints.description: Additional routing metadata (e.g. preferred channel identifiers)

quiet.description: When true, suppress completion notifications for this schedule. The job still runs and produces output, but no notification or conversation message is sent on completion. Useful for high-frequency recurring jobs that report findings separately. Defaults to false.

reuse_conversation.description: When true, reuse the same conversation across recurring schedule runs instead of creating a new one each time. Useful for polling-style schedules that accumulate context over time. Ignored for one-shot schedules. Defaults to false.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retries after the initial execution fails. Defaults to 3.

retry_backoff_ms.description: Base backoff delay in milliseconds between retries. Exponential backoff is applied. Defaults to 60000.

tools.name: schedule_list
tools.description: List scheduled automations (recurring cron/RRULE schedules and one-time scheduled events), or show details and recent runs for a specific one. For the user's task queue, use task_list_show instead.

enabled_only.description: When true, only show enabled jobs. Defaults to false.

job_id.description: If provided, show detailed info and recent runs for this specific job.

tools.name: schedule_update
tools.description: Update an existing scheduled automation (expression, syntax, message, name, enabled state, mode, or routing)

job_id.description: The ID of the schedule to update

syntax.description: Schedule syntax type. Auto-detected if omitted.

message.description: New message to send when triggered (for execute/notify modes)

script.description: New shell command to run when triggered (for script mode)

mode.description: Whether to notify the user, execute autonomously via LLM, or run a shell command directly

routing_intent.description: How to route the triggered message across channels

routing_hints.description: Additional routing metadata (e.g. preferred channel identifiers)

quiet.description: When true, suppress completion notifications for this schedule. Useful for high-frequency jobs that report findings separately.

reuse_conversation.description: When true, reuse the same conversation across recurring schedule runs instead of creating a new one each time. Useful for polling-style schedules that accumulate context over time. Ignored for one-shot schedules.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retries after the initial execution fails. Defaults to 3.

retry_backoff_ms.description: Base backoff delay in milliseconds between retries. Exponential backoff is applied. Defaults to 60000.

tools.name: schedule_delete
tools.description: Delete a scheduled automation and all its run history

job_id.description: The ID of the schedule to delete
